title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: - **High — Runtime baseline contradiction (`Claude` required vs not required).** [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md):39-43 defines Kernel quorum with `Claude` optional and `Codex + GLM + Gemini-cli` as default. [run-kernel-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/scripts/local/run-kernel-orchestration.sh):107-116 hard-requires matrix providers `codex, claude, glm`. This is a direct policy drift. - **High — Fallback quorum drift (`claude -> gemini-cli`) accepted even when not aligned with adapter quorum rules.** [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md):39-41 constrains allowed quorums and conditions. [kernel-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/kernel-lane-bridge.mjs):99-102 and [kernel-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/kernel-lane-bridge.mjs):1286-1304 allow `claude` lane fallback to `gemini-cli`. [test-run-kernel-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/tests/test-run-kernel-orchestration.sh):197-212 explicitly validates this path as success. Net effect: tooling accepts fallback shapes not clearly permitted by adapter policy text. - **Medium — `acpx` transport changes availability semantics, not just transport.** [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md):43 says `acpx` changes transport only. [kernel-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/kernel-lane-bridge.mjs):1056-1060 marks `claude` unavailable when `acpx` is requested but missing, and [kernel-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/kernel-lane-bridge.mjs):1230-1258 exits unavailable before fallback flow. This makes `acpx` opt-in materially affect lane availability/policy outcome, not only transport.
